article_meta.title: Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores 02:12 â¢ Source: CNN Classes o

reasoning: The model returned an empty array. The article is largely composed of quoted/attributed material from NWEA and its researchers. The author's own prose is relatively minimal and mostly neutral connecting language. However, there are a few arguable bias instances: (1) The headline 'Classes of more than 70 students amid historic declines in test scores' uses 'historic' which is a subjective qualifying adjective in author prose, though this could be defended as factual if the declines are indeed unprecedented. (2) The author's uncritical reproduction of NWEA's self-description as 'a pioneer in educational research and assessment methodology' without any balancing context could be seen as Elite bias or Slant. (3) The article only presents one source (NWEA) with no alternative viewpoints on the study's methodology or conclusions, which could constitute Bias by Omission. These are somewhat borderline instances, and the empty array is defensible but not perfect.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model did not flag any quoted text — no attribution rule violations since the output is empty.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No items were flagged, so no false positives exist.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed a few arguable instances: the uncritical inclusion of NWEA's self-description as 'a pioneer,' the single-source framing with no alternative perspectives on the study (potential Bias by Omission), and the use of 'historic' in the headline. These are borderline but at least the single-source issue and the uncritical self-description are defensible flags.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No labels to evaluate since the output is empty.
